title: Quizeen – Interactive Quiz Taking Platform
excerpt: I engineered Quizeen to solve a real problem: most quiz platforms force learners to lose progress if their connection hiccups. I collaborated to build an engaging, game-like experience using Next.js and Redux, implementing production-grade security and deterministic state management. The result: a fast, responsive platform where learners stay engaged and creators get deep insights into performance.
problem: Most quiz platforms are rigid, dropping users' progress if their connection stutters or providing zero analytics for the creator. We wanted a seamless, highly engaging experience that feels more like a game than a test, while giving creators deep insights.
goal: Build an engaging, highly responsive interactive quiz engine that feels like a modern SaaS platform, supporting complex state management for customizable quiz flows.
architecture: Next.js App Router for SEO and initial load speed, paired with a distinct Redux Toolkit store for managing complex, deeply nested quiz states safely.
challenges: Synchronizing complex quiz state (timer, current question, selection) with rapid page transitions without losing data or causing hydration mismatches.
solutions: Implemented a rigid deterministic state machine within Redux, ensuring the quiz flow strictly adheres to the schema. Persisted state to local storage to survive accidental reloads.
tradeOffs: Sacrificed extreme offline capabilities (PWA) in favor of a simpler, always-connected architecture to prevent state desync with the server.
lessons: Complex global state in Next.js requires careful segregation between server components and client state boundaries.
outcome: Delivered a lightweight, incredibly fast quiz taker with sub 100ms interaction latency, resulting in a streamlined testing experience.

title: WorkUp Cards – Business Card Generator
excerpt: Physical business cards become obsolete the moment they're printed. I engineered WorkUp Cards to help professionals generate, customize, and instantly download high-resolution cards without design skills. Built with React and HTML-to-image rendering, it showcases my ability to craft fast, accessible tools that solve real user problems through responsive design and intuitive interfaces.
problem: Physical business cards are outdated the moment they are printed, and manually sharing details is clumsy. We needed a frictionless way for professionals to generate, share, and update their digital presence instantly via QR codes without needing to download another app.
goal: Create a fast, client-side only business card generator capable of rendering high-resolution, print-ready outputs instantly.
architecture: React 19 with Tailwind for rapid styling. React Hook Form with Zod for robust input validation. HTML2Canvas for the heavy lifting of DOM-to-Image translation.
challenges: Rendering complex CSS (gradients, shadows, custom fonts) accurately into an image canvas without visual corruption or extreme performance lag.
solutions: Implemented a hidden rendering DOM layer that strips non-essential interactive elements and pre-loads fonts before triggering the HTML2Canvas snapshot.
tradeOffs: Restricted the use of certain hyper-complex CSS filters (like backdrop-blur) because canvas rendering engines struggle to parse them identically to the browser.
lessons: The DOM is not a 1:1 map to a Canvas context. Client-side image generation requires strict styling constraints.
outcome: A fully functional, instant-download design tool with zero server-side processing costs.

title: WorldTimeSage
excerpt: Managing global teams across timezones is exhausting. I built WorldTimeSage to help teams visualize exactly when everyone is active. Engineered as a PWA with Redux for complex timezone state, Fuse.js for rapid searching, and robust offline capabilities, it demonstrates my commitment to solving real operational challenges with thoughtful, user-centered design.
problem: Managing global teams often feels like a guessing game of timezones, leading to missed meetings and delayed responses. The goal was to eliminate timezone fatigue entirely by giving users a clean, instant visual dashboard of exactly when their team is active.
goal: Develop a comprehensive time-tracking and documentation hub offering offline capabilities and timezone customization without bloated UI.
architecture: React and Redux Toolkit for complex timezone state, Fuse.js for rapid localized searching, and a strict PWA implementation for offline access.
challenges: JavaScript's native Date object is infamously difficult for complex timezone permutations, especially with daylight savings rules changing dynamically.
solutions: Leveraged Date-fns purely for formatting while maintaining a strict UTC-first data model. Built a robust caching layer to ensure core functionality remained during offline periods.
tradeOffs: The robust documentation system meant the bundle size was larger initially, requiring aggressive code splitting at the route level.
lessons: A documentation-first approach (writing the docs alongside the code) forces you to build more intuitive UIs.
outcome: An accessible, installable PWA that functions flawlessly without an internet connection.

title: AsterMail
excerpt: I designed and built AsterMail as a flexible, production-grade email delivery service engineered for reliability and scalability. With dynamic SMTP configuration, robust validation pipelines, and comprehensive error handling, it showcases my backend expertise: designing secure APIs, implementing scalable transport logic, and building systems ready for real-world SaaS environments where delivery reliability matters.

title: Geemex – Gaming Landing Page Template
excerpt:
Geemex is an immersive gaming landing page template built with Vue.js, designed to showcase a futuristic survival game. The template highlights intense gameplay, tactical combat, cooperative multiplayer, hero stories, and in-game collectibles, offering a fully responsive and visually engaging user experience.

It demonstrates my ability to create interactive, high-performance frontend templates with modern UI design, clear content hierarchy, pricing tiers, subscription options, and promotional sections for gaming projects.

title: GitFinder – GitHub User Finder
excerpt: GitFinder is a simple and intuitive GitHub user search application built using HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and Bootstrap. Users can quickly look up GitHub profiles, view public repositories, and get basic information about developers. The project demonstrates frontend development skills, responsive design, and dynamic data handling using APIs without a backend.

title: Anonfly – Anonymous Encrypted Messaging
excerpt: Anonfly addresses a critical need: people avoid sharing honest feedback if they don't trust their anonymity. I engineered a 'Privacy by Design' platform using end-to-end encryption and cryptographic identities, ensuring conversations remain confidential without storing PII. Built with Node.js and React, featuring real-time WebSockets and IndexedDB for client-side key management, it demonstrates deep expertise in security-first architecture and complex state synchronization.
problem: People hesitate to share honest feedback if they feel their identity isn't secure. Standard forms don't guarantee anonymity. We needed to build a space where users could send truly anonymous, encrypted feedback without the anxiety of being tracked.
goal: Create a 'Privacy by Design' messaging platform utilizing cryptographic identities and end-to-end encryption, requiring absolutely no PII (Personally Identifiable Information).
architecture: Node.js/Express backend acting solely as a relay. WebSockets and SSE for real-time delivery. IndexedDB for local cryptographic key and message storage. React on the frontend.
challenges: Handling disconnected states where a user goes offline during an encrypted key exchange, leading to dropped message handshakes.
solutions: Built a robust Redis-backed message queueing system that holds encrypted payloads temporarily until the recipient websocket re-connects and acknowledges receipt.
tradeOffs: No multi-device sync. Because keys are generated and stored strictly in the local browser's IndexedDB, sessions cannot be easily transferred without explicit manual key export.
lessons: Cryptography in the browser is powerful but unforgiving. State machines handling connection drops must be bulletproof.
outcome: A fully functional, E2EE real-time chat application where the server literally cannot read the messages it relays.

title: Asterixh School Portal – Educational Hub
excerpt: A modern educational portal designed to centralize academic resources, admission processes, and school communications. It provides a seamless interface for students, parents, and staff to access curriculum details, news, and administrative notices.

title: SMS – School Management System
excerpt: School administrators waste countless hours bouncing between disjointed systems to track grades and fees. I collaborated to engineer SMS as a unified, secure platform with granular role-based access control. Using Supabase RLS policies and React Query for state management, I built a system where teachers control only their classes, students see only their records, and admins have full visibility over institutional operations.
problem: School administrators are forced to bounce between disjointed systems just to track student grades and fees. It is exhausting and prone to constant data errors. We needed to build one single, reliable source of truth for the entire institution.
goal: Centralize all operations into a single, high-performance platform featuring strict Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).
architecture: Supabase for a scalable, secure Postgres backend. Node.js/Express for custom business logic. React frontend using Zustand for state and React Query for server state caching.
challenges: Managing deeply granular permissions (e.g., a teacher can edit grades only for their assigned class, while admins can edit all, and students can only view).
solutions: Implemented Row Level Security (RLS) policies directly within Supabase alongside application-layer middleware in Express to double-verify mutation intents.
tradeOffs: Heavier initial setup time and steep learning curve for the Supabase RLS policies compared to a traditional ORM approach.
lessons: Pushing security constraints down to the database layer drastically reduces application-level bugs regarding unauthorized data access.
outcome: A production-grade, secure dashboard capable of handling thousands of relational records seamlessly.

title: Resin Art – Creative Portfolio & Catalog
excerpt: I built this creative portfolio as a visually engaging showcase combining immersive gallery design with real-time Instagram feeds and WhatsApp integration. The result demonstrates my ability to craft responsive, conversion-focused experiences that blend aesthetics with practical business functionality, helping handcrafted resin pieces reach customers and drive direct sales.
